user_query:
I exported a few text/JSON logs that mirror our local agent system. Please build a single consolidated monitoring snapshot JSON that our internal dashboard can render. Read only the files under input/ and write the result to output/dashboard_snapshot.json.

Inputs to read from input/:
- agents.json — current agents, their models, context windows, used tokens, and status
- sessions.json — recent sessions for each agent
- costs.jsonl — per-interaction cost records with timestamps and model info
- messages.jsonl — message records with timestamps, roles, and token counts
- cron.json — cron jobs with schedules and recent run timestamps
- memory_files.json — list of memory files with agent ownership and sizes
- logs.jsonl — tail of recent logs

Build output/dashboard_snapshot.json with this exact structure and rules:
1) agents: an object keyed by agent id. Each value must include:
   - model (string)
   - status (string)
   - sessions_count (integer, count from sessions.json)
   - context_used_ratio (number rounded to 4 decimals) computed as context_used_tokens/context_window from agents.json
2) costs: object with three aggregates derived from costs.jsonl (cost_usd fields):
   - daily: map of YYYY-MM-DD → string with two decimals (sum for that day)
   - monthly: map of YYYY-MM → string with two decimals (sum for that month)
   - by_model: map of model → string with two decimals (total across all records)
   Treat every cost_usd as a decimal currency value; format all results as strings with exactly two decimal places.
3) activity: object with:
   - messages_per_day: map of YYYY-MM-DD → integer (count of message records on that day from messages.jsonl)
   - token_trend: array of objects sorted by date ascending, each: { "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "tokens": total_tokens_that_day } where total_tokens_that_day is the sum of the tokens field from messages.jsonl for that date.
4) cron: array of jobs derived from cron.json, each object:
   - name (string)
   - schedule (string)
   - last_run (ISO timestamp string, the latest in last_runs)
   - runs_per_day: map of YYYY-MM-DD → integer (counts of last_runs on that day)
5) memory: object with two maps built from memory_files.json:
   - per_agent_file_count: agent → integer count of files
   - per_agent_total_bytes: agent → integer sum of size_bytes
6) sessions: object with:
   - top_sessions: array of exactly 2 session objects with the highest messages_count from sessions.json (tie-break by id ascending). Each includes id, agent, messages_count.
7) logs: object with:
   - last_entries: array of the 5 most recent entries from logs.jsonl sorted by ts descending. Each item must include ts, agent, level, message.

Do not add extra fields. Use only the provided files; no heuristics. Write the consolidated JSON to output/dashboard_snapshot.json.
